sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

keyPoints.text: 2026년 1분기 신규 수주가 183억 달러로 유기적 기준 전년보다 71% 급증하며 모든 사업부가 성장했다.
수주잔고가 분기 대비 130억 달러 늘었고, 회사는 강한 수요를 근거로 2026년 실적 가이던스를 상향했다.
매출은 93억 달러로 16% 늘었고, 잉여현금흐름은 48억 달러로 전년의 네 배를 넘어섰다.

businessChanges.text: 데이터센터向 장비 주문이 24억 달러로 작년 한 해 전체를 넘어섰고, 가스터빈 수주잔고·슬롯예약은 연말까지 110 GW 도달이 예상된다.

financialHighlights.text: 매출 93억 달러는 전년보다 16%(유기적 7%) 늘었으며, 전력·전기화 부문 장비가 성장을 견인했다.
전력(Power) 부문 수주는 100억 달러로 59% 늘었고, 전기화(Electrification)는 71억 달러로 86% 증가하며 수주잔고 확대를 이끌었다.
순이익률은 50.9%였으나, 여기에는 Prolec GE 관련 일회성 세전 M&A 차익이 포함됐다.

watchItems.text: 순이익률 50.9%에는 Prolec GE 관련 일회성 M&A 차익이 크게 반영된 만큼, 다음 분기부터 경상적 수익성이 어느 수준에서 자리잡는지 확인할 필요가 있다.
연말 가스터빈 수주잔고·슬롯예약 110 GW 목표가 제시된 만큼, 데이터센터 수요가 실제 장비 출하로 이어지는 속도를 지켜볼 필요가 있다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: GE Vernova reports first quarter 2026 financial results and raises 2026 guidance Strong performance with significant orders and backlog growth, margin expansion, and cash generation First Quarter 2026 Highlights: • Orders of $18.3 B, + 71% organically with growth in all segments • Backlog 1 growth of $ 13.0 B sequentially from equipment and services, including $5B from Prolec GE • Gas Power equipment backlog and slot reservation agreements grew from 83 to 100 GW; now anticipate reaching at least 110 GW by year-end 2026 • Revenue of $9.3 B, + 16% , + 7% organically* led by equipment at Electrification and Power • Net income of $4.7 B; net income margin of 50.9% ; inclusive of $4.5 B pre-tax M&A net gains, primarily from Prolec GE • Adjusted EBITDA* of $0.9 B , nearly doubling year-over-year; adjusted EBITDA margin* of 9.6  Normalized numeric aliases: $18.3 = 18.3달러; $13.0 = 13.0달러; $5 = 5달러; $9.3 = 9.3달러; $4.7 = 4.7달러; $4.5 = 4.5달러; $0.9 = 0.9달러; $5.2 = 5.2달러; 390 basis points = 390포인트.

B; free cash flow* of $ 4.8 B, more than quadrupling year-over-year • $10.2 B cash balance; $1.4B in capital returned to shareholders CAMBRIDGE, Mass., (April 22, 2026) – GE Vernova Inc. (NYSE: GEV), a unique industry leader enabling customers to accelerate the energy transition, today reported financial results for the first quarter ending March 31, 2026. “ We had a solid start to 2026 as we continue to serve the growing, long-cycle electric power market. Demand is accelerating for our Power and Electrification solutions from a diverse set of customers, with our backlog growing b y more than $13 billion quarter-over-quarter ,” said GE Vernova CEO Scott Strazik. “ Reflecting this strength, we now expect to reach at least 110 GW of combined gas turbine backlog and slot reservation agreements by year-end 2026 and are raising our 2026 financial guidance. In the quarter, our Electrif  Normalized numeric aliases: $13 billion = 130억 달러; $4.8 = 4.8달러; $10.2 = 10.2달러; $1.4 = 1.4달러; $13 = 13달러.

segment booked $2.4 billion in equipment orders to support data centers, more than all of last year. We also completed our acquisition of the remaining fifty percent stake in Prolec GE, a leading grid equipment supplier, strengthening our ability to serve customers and accelerating our growth trajectory. Our team is executing well and remains focused on delivering for the long-term . ” In the quarter, orders of $18.3 billion increased + 71% organically, with strong equipment growth in Electrification and Power, and services growth led by Power . Revenue of $9.3 billion was up + 16% , + 7% organically *, with strong equipment growth at Electrification and Power, along with higher services, partially offset by Wind. Margins expanded significantly from price, volume, and productivity. Free cash flow* of $4.8 bi  Normalized numeric aliases: $2.4 billion = 24억 달러; $18.3 billion = 183억 달러; $9.3 billion = 93억 달러; $4.8 billion = 48억 달러; $2.4 = 2.4달러; $18.3 = 18.3달러; $9.3 = 9.3달러; $4.8 = 4.8달러.

ositive benefits from working capital and stronger adjusted EBITDA*. Power • Orders of $10.0 billion increased + 59% organically and revenues of $5.0 billion increased + 12% , + 10% organically*, led by Gas Power equipment . Segment EBITDA margin grew + 470 basis points, + 500 basis points organically* . • Signed 21 gigawatts (GW) of new gas equipment contracts, including 19 GW of slot reservation agreements and 2 GW of orders. Converted 6 GW of existing slot reservation agreements to orders and shipped 4 GW of equipment; resulting in backlog growth from 40 to 44 GW and an increase in slot reservation agreements from 43 to 56 GW.
